coredata.dc:title: Novel manganese carbon quantum dots as a nano-probe: Facile synthesis, characterization and their application in naproxen delivery (Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen)
coredata.dc:creator: Ardestani, Mehdi Shafiee
Zaheri, Zahra
Mohammadzadeh, Pardis
Bitarafan-Rajabi, Ahmad
Ghoreishi, Seyedeh Masoumeh
coredata.dc:description: This study for the first time pursues two crucial aims of using Naproxen as a non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug in a better, non-invasive setting and introducing a simple and biocompatible nano-carrier (Mn/CQD/SiO2) which is a magneto carbon quantum dots modified with mesoporous silica probe which can be served as a drug delivery and tracer system. SiO2 modification was doneby mesoporous silica which improves biocompatibility and provideslow cytotoxicity. Naproxen was conjugated to the nano-probe to form Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen and biodistribution was investigated. Physicochemical characteristics of the Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen were investigated using FT-IR, SEM, TEM, UV–Vis and BET. Antiproliferation assay using MTT assay was performed on HEK-293 cells to determine the cytotoxity of Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen. Relaxivity of Mn/CQD/SiO2 was examined thereafter. To investigate the imaging capability of Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen and biodistribution of Naproxen, fluorescent imaging was done. To confirm the data, then the levels of COX Gene expression was determined. The specific surface area, pore volume, and pore radius were 44.4 m2/g, 10.23 cm3/g, and 25.9 nm respectively. MTT assay showed no cytotoxicity. Relaxivity of Mn/CQD/SiO2 was higher than conventional Gd-based contrast agent. Fluorescence imaging of Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen showed the biodistribution of naproxen. COX Gene expression confirmed the biodistribution data. By increasing the accumulation in liver COX production reduced. All in all, unique features of Mn/CQD/SiO2 including biocompatibility, low toxicity, magnetic and fluorescence properties showed that it can be used in biomedical sciences.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Nanoparticle
Biocompatible
Molecular imaging
Fluorescence imaging
MR imaging

xocs:funding.xocs:funding-agency: Tehran University of Medical Sciences and Health Services

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: Tehran University of Medical Sciences supported this study. The authors wish to thank all the technicians who provided support during the experiments.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: FT-IR spectrum of CQD (a), CQD/SiO2 (b), Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen (c).
UV.Vis spectroscopy of Mn/CQD and Mn/CQD/SiO2.
(a) SEM micrograph of Mn/CQD/SiO2, (b) TEM image of Mn/CQD/SiO2 and (c) EDS mapping analysis of Mn/CQD/SiO2.
Size and zeta potential of Mn/CQD/SiO2.
BET analysis of Mn/CQD/SiO2. Specific surface area, pore volume, and pore radius were 44.4 m2/g, 10.23 Cm3/g, and 25.9 nm respectively.
MTT assay: HEK-293 cells were treated by Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen for 24 h.
Magnetic properties of Mn/CQD and Mn/CQD/SiO2.
Biodistribution and COX gene expression of Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen in different organ at 90 min after injection.
Preparation path of magneto carbone quantum dot.
preparation path of magneto carbone quantum dot modifed by SiO2 and conjugation with naproxen.

head.ce:title: Novel manganese carbon quantum dots as a nano-probe: Facile synthesis, characterization and their application in naproxen delivery (Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen)

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: This study for the first time pursues two crucial aims of using Naproxen as a non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug in a better, non-invasive setting and introducing a simple and biocompatible nano-carrier (Mn/CQD/SiO2) which is a magneto carbon quantum dots modified with mesoporous silica probe which can be served as a drug delivery and tracer system. SiO2 modification was doneby mesoporous silica which improves biocompatibility and provideslow cytotoxicity. Naproxen was conjugated to the nano-probe to form Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen and biodistribution was investigated. Physicochemical characteristics of the Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen were investigated using FT-IR, SEM, TEM, UV–Vis and BET. Antiproliferation assay using MTT assay was performed on HEK-293 cells to determine the cytotoxity of Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen. Relaxivity of Mn/CQD/SiO2 was examined thereafter. To investigate the imaging capability of Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen and biodistribution of Naproxen, fluorescent imaging was done. To confirm the data, then the levels of COX Gene expression was determined. The specific surface area, pore volume, and pore radius were 44.4 m2/g, 10.23 cm3/g, and 25.9 nm respectively. MTT assay showed no cytotoxicity. Relaxivity of Mn/CQD/SiO2 was higher than conventional Gd-based contrast agent. Fluorescence imaging of Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen showed the biodistribution of naproxen. COX Gene expression confirmed the biodistribution data. By increasing the accumulation in liver COX production reduced. All in all, unique features of Mn/CQD/SiO2 including biocompatibility, low toxicity, magnetic and fluorescence properties showed that it can be used in biomedical sciences.

ce:section.ce:para: Nanotechnology is a developing science that uses material in 1–100 nm at least in one dimension. Nanotechnology has influenced different aspects of human life [1–4] and developed in areas [5–8]. Scientists have considered nanotechnology for developing new drug delivery systems to overwhelmed the limitation of convectional formulation [9–14]. Nanotechnology based drug delivery systems, which are under research and evaluation, includes dendrimers, micelles, carbon and quantum dots, and liposomes [15–21]. These types of nanomaterials have improved in vast majority aspects of science, and their applications in medicine and health so-called “nanomedicine” has fascinated researchers. Furthermore, prevention, detection, and treatment of diseases at early stage and also follow-up of diseases have been possible by developing the nanomedicine [13,22]. The most beneficial use of nanoparticles is their applications in drug delivery systems. In this technique lower dose of drugs compare to traditional formulation is used and thus side effects of drugs are reduced dramatically. Moreover, high selectivity of drug delivery based nanomaterials meaning deposition in lesion causes a reduction in pain and costs [23]. Molecular imaging is another area where nanomaterials are used. MRI, Ultrasonic, Fluorescence, CT, SPECT, and PET imaging are common imaging approaches in which nano tools are being developed [24,25].

Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is a noninvasive diagnostic technique for detecting abnormalities without damaging ionizing radiation. Therefore, MRI is a technique that can be used in people who might be vulnerable to radiation, e.g. children and pregnant women [26]. MRI contrast agents such as gadolinium, manganese, and iron oxide have been widely used in clinical and preclinical studies. Among these, contrast agents based on gadolinium have been applied widespread. Gd3+ based MR imaging probes have been limited due to the safety concern of side effect of free Gd3+ in renal compromised patients. Furthermore, deposition of gadolinium in the cerebellum structures has been reported after repeated administrations of gadolinium-based contrast agent [27]. Thus, improvements of safe alternative imaging probes that are compatible to impact patient outcomes are essential. In this framework, several imaging agents have been considered as substitutions, but all had disadvantages. Manganese (Mn2+) known as a non-lanthanide metal has been considered as an alternative contrast agent to Gd3+, which is essential in cell biology, represents non-toxic paramagnetic MRI contrast agent [28]. Compare to Gd3+, Mn2+ is a nutritional element that human body requires and it exhibits all of the physical properties that Gd3+ have. Since Gd3+ has some drawbacks, studies on manganese-based MRI contrast agent have been intensified [29].

In a biological system unique characteristics of nanoparticles such as high surface to volume ratio, and small size are main features that allow us to use them as a tracer [30]. Among nanoparticles, quantum Dots (QDs) are semiconductor nanoparticles that have been used in molecular imaging since two decades ago [31,32]. Although, unique features of QDs including reduced light scattering and low tissue absorption have fascinated researchers, cytotoxicity of heavy metals which are used in conventional QDs have restricted their applications in medicine [33]. Thus, designing new QDs which are biodegradable and ecologically safe are so important. Carbone quantum dots are prominent agents that can be used in both treatment and imaging. This metallic nanoparticle has considered for their benefit features such as earth abundant, nontoxic, ecologically safe, biodegradable, and fluorescent properties. Physical characteristic including highest emission wavelength at 500 nm and strong emission wavelength at 600 nm made it as suitable agent for in-vivo imaging [34].

In this work, we designed the novel magneto carbon quantum dots modified with mesoporous silica probe for investigation of naproxen (Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen). Naproxen is one of the non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) which is used against different diseases such as in autoimmune diseases and chronic inflammations e.g. rheumatoid arthritis. However, its use in elderly population might lead to other serious and fatal complications. Therefore, biodistribution of naproxen by a new non-invasive method is very practical [35]. Developing multifunctional nanostructures remain a challenging issue and a great deal of research have been done in this framework [36–38]. In this study, mesoporous silica was chosen because it can be modified simply by several functional groups, and it can conjugate drugs easily. Moreover, modification of mesoporous can increase biocompatibility [39]. In this study, for the first time, we introduced a new nano-system that has the potential feature for magnetic (MRI) and optical imaging and for further study we examined this novel nano-carrier to investigate naproxen in vivo.

All chemical reagents were of analytical grade and used without further purification. Manganese acetate, (3-Aminopropyl) triethoxysilane (APTES), tetraethyl orthosilicate (TEOS), cetyltrimethylammonium bromide (CTAB), dicyclohexylcarbodiimide (DCC), naproxen, urea, and citric acid were purchased from Sigma-Aldrich Co. (St Louis, MO, USA). Dimethylformamide was purchased from Merck. HEK-293 cell line was obtained from the Pasteur Institute (Tehran, Islamic Republic of Iran). Fourier transform infrared spectra (FT-IR) of the powders acquired using Perkin Elmer Spectrum BX-II spectrometer. The UV–Vis absorption spectra were recorded from 200 to 600 nm by an Ultraviolet–Visible spectrophotometer by Shimadzu; UV-3101PC; Japan. 3D and 2D fluorescence spectroscopy were done by Nanoimager and FluoVision instrument respectively. MRI imagines were performed by Siemens instrument. Surface morphology of the products was investigated using scanning electron microscopy (SEM, LEO-1455VP) and transmission electron microscopy (TEM, JEM-2100). Dynamic light scattering (DLS) was measured by Malvern nano-zs. Determination of quantum yield was performed by qualaquin sulfate as control. Surface area was determined by Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) method. Excitation and emission of the solution was measured by UV–Vis. All studies were repeated three times. Studies were directed in line with the principles of declaration of Tehran university of medical sciences with the number: IR.TUMS.TIPS.REC.1397.086.

The MTT assay protocol is based on the conversion of water soluble MTT (3-(4,5-dimethylthiazol-2-yl)-2,5-diphenyltetrazolium bromide) compound to an insoluble formazan product.On the first day, HEK-293 cells resuspended in DEMEM media, were seeded in a 96-well plate (10,000 cells/100 μl Media/well) and maintained in the CO2 incubator at 37 °C overnight. The following day, cells were treated with Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen and incubated for 24 h. Thereafter, 10 µl MTT solution (final concentration: 0.5 mg/ml) was added and cells were again incubated for 2 h at 37 °C in the dark. Further, the media was carefully removed, and cells were dissolved in 100 µl of DMSO/well by pipetting up and down multiple times and incubated for 15 mins at 37 °C. This results in the formation of Formazan crystals in the cells. The absorbance was then read at 570 nm on a multiwall plate reader. MTT assay was done in the triple set.

Mn/CQD and Mn/CQD/SiO2 (20 mg) were dissolved in deionized water (100 ml) at 0.005, 0.01, 0.5, 1, 2 mg/ml different concentrations. For noise attenuation for each row of product one row of distilled water was added. MRI images were carried out and data were analysis by J image software.

3 BALB/c mice (22 g) were chosen for fluorescence imaging and 0.3 ml of the Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen was injected to anesthetized mice. Injected mice were lied down and in-vivo imaging was done by optical spectroscopy. For further investigation biodistribution study was performed. Mice were killed and heart, kidney, lung, and liver were harvested and washed with normal saline and accumulation of Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen in each organ was measured by optical imaging. Percentage dose was obtained from accumulation determined (per gram of tissue) in each organ divided by total injection.

Statistical analysis was done by Prism9, MATLAB, Microsoft office (2013). For quantitative data analysis one-way analysis of variance followed by Tukey’s test was applied. P < 0.05 was considered statistically significant.

Although contrast agents based on gadolinium are being used in clinic normally, safety concern in renal impaired patient is still an important challenge. Manganese based contrast agents represent a feasible way to provide contrast-enhanced images in patients using iodinated CT or gadolinium MR imaging. Although NSF is a main concern in gadolinium based contrast agent, no relation between NSF and manganese based contrast agent has been found [26,40]. Manganese complexes have similar pharmaco- kinetic and dynamic behavior to gadolinium complexes. Biodistribution of free ion (Mn2+ and Gd3+) is the key difference between gadolinium and manganese based contrast agent. Free Mn2+ has an efficient elimination route through hepatobiliary system which does not exist for Gd3+ [41]. In this work for the first time, we designed the Mn/CQD/SiO2 which can be used as an alternative for Gd3+ contrast agent. For further investigation, Naproxen (an anti-inflammatory drug) was conjugated to Mn/CQD/SiO2 and the biodistribution of conjugate was studies thereafter. Subsequently, nano-probe was synthesized, and analysis confirmed by spectroscopy.

FT-IR spectrometers of CQD (a), CQD/SiO2 (b), and Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen (c) are illustrated in Fig. 1 , which were used to confirm the functional groups. In Fig. 1a, wide peaks at 3000 to 3500 cm−1 belong to –NH and –OH acidic groups. CH stretching appeared at 3000 cm−1. The peak that located about 1600 cm−1 belongs to carbonyl group. All peaks that are related to CQD can also be observed in Fig. 1b but a litter sharper that might be due to the modification of SiO2 on CQD. The sharp peak about 1000 cm−1 observed in Fig. 1b belongs to Si-O group [42]. Fig. 1c presents a FT-IR spectrum of Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen. The wide peak at 3500 cm−1 is related to OH/NH groups. 1600 cm−1 is because of carbonyl groups. Absorption bands at 3000 and 1000 cm−1 are related to CH and Si-O stretching [42,43]. For further study, UV–Vis spectroscopy of Mn/CQD and Mn/CQD/SiO2 was performed and data is presented in Fig. 2 . The emission spectra of Mn/CQD showed the typical wavelength which is the nature of CQDs [44]. Moreover, Maximum emission was detected at 525 nm. In contrast to Mn/CQD, Mn/CQD/SiO2 showed reduce absorption which confirms the modification of nanoparticles.

Porous structure of Mn/CQD/SiO2 is one of the significant parameters which examine the adsorption and desorption abilities. Surface area, pore radius, and pore volume have potential effect on drug delivery. Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) analysis was done to investigate the characteristic of Mn/CQD/SiO2. According to the BET analysis, the specific surface area was estimated 44.4 m2/g with pore volume 10.23 cm3/g, and pore radius 25.9 nm. Adsorption and desorption diagram is illustrated in Fig. 5 . BET adsorption and desorption isotherm show a sharp rise in the low-pressure p/p0 < 1, and the presence of an inflexion around p/p0 = 0.6 is obvious.

Cell viability of Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen on HEK-293 cell lines was performed using MTT assay (Fig. 6 ). Incubation of HEK-293 at different concentrations (10, 25, 50, 100, 250, 500, and 1000 µg/mL) of Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen after 24 h showed the dose-dependence decrease in cell survival compare to control group. In addition, result showed that no differences were observed between treated and control groups. Pervious study reported that Mn/CQD did not present any toxic effect up to 500 µg/mL [44]. In our work, modification with mesoporous silica led to increase in safety concentration of Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen up to 1000 µg/mL by improving biocompatibility and lowered cytotoxicity [45]. At 1000 µg/mL concentration of Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen, percentage of viable cells were approximately 90 of total population. Means that no-cytotoxicity was shown at this concentration. Result obtained from cell survival assay exhibited that Mn/CQD/SiO2 is a good carrier for imaging and maybe a suitable delivery system for in-vivo environment.

Relaxivity of Mn/CQD and Mn/CQD/SiO2 at 1.5 T were calculated from the linear correlation between relaxation rate and different concentrations (Fig. 7 ). The R1 values were 4.12 and 5.82 nM-1S-1 for Mn/CQD and Mn/CQD/SiO2 respectively. For the first time we report R1 values that is suitable for bright contrast MR imaging. Obtained R1 value in our work was higher than conventional free gadobutrol, which is 2.8 mM-1S-1, reported in previous study [46]. Also, relaxation time of Mn/CQD/SiO2 (5.82 nM-1S-1) was higher than Mn/CQD (3.98 mM-1S-1) which is reported previously [47]. Our result clearly indicates that this novel Mn/CQD/SiO2 is a good agent for MR imaging.

The main propose of the current study is to design the novel Mn/CQD/SiO2 for dual bio-imaging. Fig. 8 a shows in-vivo fluorescence imaging was performed at 15, 30, 45, 60, 90, and 120 min after injection. It is obvious that high accumulation of Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen occurred at 90 min after injection. Fluorescence imaging in liver, lung, kidney, and heart at 90 min is presented in Fig. 8b. Biodistribution of Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen and COX gene expression in heart, kidney, lung, and liver after 90 min is illustrated in Fig. 9 . Naproxen is a pain palliation drug belong to large group called NSAIDs. Naproxen works by blocking the cyclooxygenase (COX) causes to reduce prostaglandin which is a chemical substance responsible for pain and inflammatory response in the body. As it is clear in the Fig. 9, by increasing the Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen accumulation, COX gene expression was reduced, and this effect confirmed the hypothesis of naproxen mechanism. Percentage of COX production to normal uptake in organ are around 1.5, 2.3, 1.9, and 0.2 for heart, kidney, lung, and liver respectively. Based on the biodistribution study, high accumulation in liver showed that excretion route of designed Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen is through hepatobiliary system. This excretion route can be concluding by the nature of Mn/CQD nanoparticles.

All in all, results showed that Mn/CQD/SiO2 nano-carrier showed good MRI properties compare to conventional agent. Also no-cytotoxicty at higher concentration, different excertion route may provide prospective application of this novel carrier in development of nano- MRI contrast agent.

Briefly, Mn/CQD was prepared in the present of urea, manganese acetate, citric acid, and then modified by SiO2. Conjugation of naproxen was done by DCC reagent. Spectroscopies confirmed the obtained nanoparticle. Also, nano-probe was investigated for magnetic and florescence properties. MTT assay showed no cytotoxicity and biodistribution presented that Mn/CQD/SiO2@naproxen excretion route is through hepatobiliary system. Although our investigation support hypothesis that Mn/CQD/SiO2 is able to be used as an imaging contrast, more studies need to be done.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: Tehran University of Medical Sciences supported this study. The authors wish to thank all the technicians who provided support during the experiments.
